title: Missing direct file access guard
summary: PHP files in plugins should guard against direct access with an ABSPATH or equivalent check.

detection.notes: The skill currently performs a simple head-of-file check. The neutral core should formalize this with a file-aware detector.

guidance.why_it_matters: Direct file access guards help prevent PHP files from being executed outside the intended WordPress bootstrap context.
guidance.fix: Add a guard near the top of each executable PHP file, such as defined('ABSPATH') || exit;.
guidance.false_positive_risk: Medium. Not every PHP file in a plugin should be treated identically, and generated vendor code may need special handling.
